allowed_values_or_formula: Rent; Own; Live with family / do not personally rent or own; Student housing / dorm; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Determine whether the respondent has physical and purchasing control over their space.

allowed_values_or_formula: Detached house; Semi-detached house; Townhouse; Condo; Apartment; Basement apartment; Shared housing / roommate situation; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Segment house vs apartment/condo dwellers; installation tolerance, available space, decorating control, and lighting needs may differ.
Estimate household complexity and whether purchase decisions are individual or shared.

allowed_values_or_formula: Bedroom; Desk / workspace; Gaming setup; TV stand / media area; Shelf / display area; Living room; Kitchen; Dining area; Entryway / hallway; Balcony / patio; Entire home; I do not have much control over decorating; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Identify whether the respondent controls the exact spaces Lambenti is likely to occupy.

allowed_values_or_formula: Mostly me; Mostly my partner / spouse; Mostly a parent or family member; Mostly a roommate; Shared evenly between multiple people; Nobody puts much effort into decorating; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Identify who is emotionally and practically responsible for beautifying the space.

allowed_values_or_formula: Mostly me; Mostly my partner / spouse; Mostly a parent or family member; Mostly a roommate; Shared decision; Depends on the room; I usually avoid buying decorative products; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Determine who has purchasing authority for Lambenti-like products.

allowed_values_or_formula: Complete freedom; Mostly free, but I may check with someone first; Some freedom, but there are limits; Very limited freedom; Almost no freedom
analysis_notes: Assess installation freedom.

allowed_values_or_formula: Very comfortable; Somewhat comfortable; Neutral; Somewhat uncomfortable; Very uncomfortable
analysis_notes: Determine whether setup/cable management will be a blocker.

allowed_values_or_formula: numeric/open text
analysis_notes: Create an aesthetic-priority segment.

allowed_values_or_formula: Rows: Bedroom; Desk / workspace; Gaming setup; TV stand / media area; Shelf / display area; Living room. Columns: 1–10
analysis_notes: Identify which use-cases have the strongest emotional/aesthetic relevance.

allowed_values_or_formula: No; Yes
analysis_notes: Capture whether there are additional home areas where the respondent makes aesthetic/atmospheric effort; if Yes, collect Q11a text.

allowed_values_or_formula: free text; shown only if Q11 = Yes
analysis_notes: Unexpected use-case text for additional beautiful/atmospheric areas.

allowed_values_or_formula: I mostly care that it is functional; I care somewhat about how it looks, but it is not a major priority; I care about both function and appearance; I put active effort into making my space feel beautiful, cozy, or atmospheric; My living space is a major form of personal expression for me
analysis_notes: Identify whether the respondent sees space design as self-expression, atmosphere, function, or maintenance.

allowed_values_or_formula: Yes; No; Not sure / I may have bought something minor, like bulbs only
analysis_notes: Basic lighting-buyer screener. If using a simple form, do not branch respondents out; Q14 has a no-purchase option.

allowed_values_or_formula: Light bulbs or replacement bulbs; Smart bulbs or smart lighting kits; Table lamps or bedside lamps; Desk lamps or monitor lamps; Floor lamps; Ceiling fixtures, pendant lights, chandeliers, or track lights; Recessed lights / pot lights / downlights; Wall lights or sconces; Bathroom or vanity lights; Kitchen, cabinet, closet, or under-cabinet lights; LED strips or adhesive light strips; TV, monitor, gaming, or desk setup backlighting; Decorative, mood, or ambient lighting; Modular wall lights or light panels; String lights, fairy lights, or patio lights; Outdoor, landscape, porch, or security lights; Dimmers, remotes, sensors, hubs, switches, or lighting accessories; DIY lighting parts, controllers, power supplies, diffusers, or LED channels; Other: ______; I have not bought any lighting products in the past 24 months
analysis_notes: Main behavioral segmentation question. Do not collapse no-recent-purchase into weak automatically.

allowed_values_or_formula: To replace a broken or old bulb / fixture; To make a room brighter or more functional; To improve the mood or atmosphere of a space; To make a desk, TV area, gaming setup, or bedroom look better; To add smart-home control or automation; For renovation or home improvement; For outdoor, patio, or security use; For seasonal decoration; Other: ______; I have not bought lighting in the past 24 months
analysis_notes: Separate utility buyers from atmospheric buyers.

allowed_values_or_formula: numeric/open text
analysis_notes: Identify emotional product memories: glow quality, ease, aesthetics, novelty, transformation, premium feel, or convenience.

allowed_values_or_formula: It made the space look better; It changed the mood of the room; It was useful or practical; It felt premium or well-designed; It was fun or satisfying to use; It was smart / automated / app-controlled; It solved a specific problem; Other: ______; Not applicable / I did not buy lighting
analysis_notes: Understand purchase satisfaction drivers.

allowed_values_or_formula: Amazon; IKEA; Walmart; Costco; Home Depot; Lowe’s / RONA; Canadian Tire; Best Buy; Wayfair; A lighting or furniture store; A brand’s own website; TikTok Shop / Instagram / social media shop; Other online store: ______; Other physical store: ______; I do not remember; Not applicable / I did not buy lighting
analysis_notes: Determine possible acquisition channels and buyer expectations.

allowed_values_or_formula: numeric/open text
analysis_notes: Capture top-of-mind retailer recall.
Measure willingness to spend on space improvement, not only lighting.

allowed_values_or_formula: $0 CAD; $1–24 CAD; $25–49 CAD; $50–99 CAD; $100–199 CAD; $200–399 CAD; $400+ CAD; I do not remember
analysis_notes: Measure lighting-specific spending ceiling.

allowed_values_or_formula: Never; Rarely; Sometimes; Often; Very often
analysis_notes: Identify people who repeatedly buy atmosphere/aesthetic products.

allowed_values_or_formula: numeric/open text
analysis_notes: Main interest score. Primary metric: strong interest rate = % rating 6 or 7.

allowed_values_or_formula: Desk / workspace; TV stand / media area; Gaming setup; Bedroom; Shelf / display area; Living room; Office; I would not use this; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Identify most promising product use case and marketing angle.

allowed_values_or_formula: It could make my space look better; It could improve the mood or atmosphere of a room; The magnetic interaction sounds satisfying; It avoids visible buttons, remotes, or apps; It seems unique or novel; It could improve a desk, gaming, or TV setup; It could be a good gift; I like smart or unusual home products; Nothing interests me about it; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Identify value proposition strength.

allowed_values_or_formula: Price; Setup / installation; Cable management; Whether I would actually use it; Whether it would look premium; Whether the magnetic control would feel gimmicky; Durability / reliability; Whether it would fit my space; I do not like ambient lighting; I do not have concerns; Other: ______
analysis_notes: Identify objections and separate conversion barriers from target-fit killers.

allowed_values_or_formula: Under $50 CAD; $50–79 CAD; $80–109 CAD; $110–149 CAD; $150–199 CAD; $200+ CAD; I would not consider buying it
analysis_notes: Measure price readiness. Analyze price only after filtering for actual interest.

allowed_values_or_formula: I would seriously consider buying this; I would be interested, but only at the right price; I like the idea, but I am not sure I would buy it; It is interesting, but probably not for me; I am not interested
analysis_notes: Purchase intent signal.

allowed_values_or_formula: Yes; Maybe; No
analysis_notes: Measure whether visual demonstration could increase conversion.

allowed_values_or_formula: numeric/open text
analysis_notes: Collect qualitative improvement ideas.
